query: We ran 8 customer interviews. 6 said they'd pay, 2 said not now. Should we pivot or build?
expected_behavior: Flags that 'would pay' in interviews is weak evidence — asks if there are pre-orders or concrete commitments
Applies the pre-defined kill threshold if one was set, or asks what the threshold was
Produces a clear Pivot / Persevere decision framework with the evidence cited
If recommending to build, names the next Riskiest Assumption to test during build
Does not manufacture optimism — presents the ambiguity honestly
